title: How can I integrate the JIRA subtasks as Monday.com Subitems?
excerpt: I’ve been able to integrate JIRA tasks with Monday.com.  However, the subtasks are not transferred to Monday.com.  I would like to also integrate the JIRA sub tasks as Monday.com Sub tasks under the main task.  Does anyo…

posts.author: ceugrin (Claudia Eugrin)
posts.cookedHtml: I’ve been able to integrate JIRA tasks with Monday.com. However, the subtasks are not transferred to Monday.com. I would like to also integrate the JIRA sub tasks as Monday.com Sub tasks under the main task. Does anyone have a recipe that helps do this? I know sub tasks were not supported some years ago but maybe this has changed.

posts.author: Clarey (MondayMotivator)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey, I’m not an expert but here is an idea of how this could work!JIRA subtasks weren’t supported directly in the native Monday.com-JIRA integration, but that has since changed. Now, it’s possible to sync JIRA subtasks into Monday.com, although setting them up as subitems (i.e., Monday.com sub-tasks) requires a few extra steps and sometimes a workaround.

Here’s how you can approach this:

Native Integration (Updated Method)

As of the latest updates:

  1. Go to Monday.com > Integrations Center.
  2. Select JIRA Cloud or JIRA Server, depending on what you use.
  3. Choose the recipe:
  • "When an issue is created in JIRA, create an item in this board."
  • AND also add:
    "When a subtask is created in JIRA, create a subitem in this item."
    4.(Note: this depends on your plan and whether subitems are enabled.)*

If you don’t see this recipe available, you may need to:

  • Enable subitems in your board.
  • Reach out to support to enable advanced JIRA integration features.
  • Check that your JIRA issue type “sub-task” is being properly recognized (sometimes custom workflows need mapping).

Workaround Using Make (Integromat) or Zapier

If the native integration doesn’t fully support what you need:

  1. Use Make (formerly Integromat) or Zapier to:
  • Watch for new JIRA subtasks.
  • Match them with the parent task in Monday.com using a shared key (like the issue key or summary).
  • Create the subtask as a subitem under the correct Monday.com item.

Pro Tip:

Use a unique identifier from JIRA (like the issue key) in a Monday column to help pair subtasks with their parent items. It helps ensure proper matching when automating.

Let me know if you with a like if this helps or feel free to ask for more support!

posts.author: ceugrin (Claudia Eugrin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Claire, I have been working on the subtask integration and tried several different ways to add the subtasks but maybe I am missing something.

Can you tell me where can I find the recipe you mention below? We have JIRA Cloud,.

"When a subtask is created in JIRA, create a subitem in this item."
